questions.explanation: SUMIF adds values in a range that meet one specified criterion (e.g., Region equals West).
questions.question: In Excel, which function is best suited to sum values that meet a single condition (e.g., sum Sales where Region = "West")?

questions.explanation: WHERE filters individual rows prior to GROUP BY and aggregate functions; HAVING filters after aggregation.
questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter rows before aggregation occurs?

questions.explanation: dropna() removes rows (or columns) with missing values, depending on parameters.
questions.question: In pandas, which method is commonly used to remove rows containing missing values?

questions.explanation: Standard deviation quantifies typical deviation from the mean, indicating variability.
questions.question: Which statistic measures the spread of data around the mean?

questions.explanation: A LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table (Customers) and matches Orders when available; unmatched orders become NULL.
questions.question: You have a Customers table and an Orders table. Which SQL join returns all customers, including those with no orders, along with any matching orders?

questions.explanation: Fact tables contain measures (metrics) and link to dimension tables via foreign keys; dimensions hold descriptive context.

options.A: Store descriptive attributes like customer name and product category
options.B: Store transactional or measurable metrics (e.g., sales amount) and foreign keys to dimensions
options.C: Eliminate the need for dimension tables by denormalizing everything
options.D: Store only unstructured data such as text and images

questions.question: In a star schema used for analytics, what is the primary role of a fact table?

questions.explanation: CALCULATE evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, enabling dynamic measures responsive to slicers.
questions.question: In Power BI, you need a measure that recalculates based on slicer selections (filter context). Which DAX function is most commonly used to modify filter context for a calculation?

questions.explanation: Separating line items into an OrderItems table removes repeating groups (1NF), supports any number of items per order, and enables efficient joins and aggregations.

options.A: Keep the table as-is and add more ProductN columns as needed
options.B: Split into Orders (OrderID, CustomerID, OrderDate) and OrderItems (OrderID, ProductID, Quantity) with a foreign key
options.C: Store the repeating groups as a comma-separated string in one column
options.D: Move all product columns into the Customers table to reduce joins

questions.question: A database has a single Orders table with repeating groups (Product1, Qty1, Product2, Qty2, ...). Which redesign best addresses normalization and improves queryability?

questions.explanation: A raw (immutable) layer plus curated transformed tables supports auditability, reprocessing, late data handling, and performant querying via incremental loads and partitioning.

options.A: Overwrite the only table each day with the latest extract and keep no history
options.B: Load raw immutable data to a landing zone, then transform into curated tables with incremental processing and partitioning by event_date
options.C: Manually copy/paste daily CSVs into Excel and publish the workbook as the source of truth
options.D: Store all events only as images to reduce schema management

questions.question: You must build a daily pipeline that ingests app events, supports late-arriving data, and allows analysts to query both raw and cleaned data. Which approach best fits common data engineering best practices?

questions.explanation: Text must be represented numerically (TF-IDF/embeddings) before applying mining methods like clustering or topic modeling to extract themes from unstructured data.

options.A: Use k-means directly on raw text strings without preprocessing
options.B: Convert text to numeric features (e.g., TF-IDF or embeddings), then apply clustering or topic modeling to identify themes
options.C: Only compute mean and standard deviation of ticket length to find issues
options.D: Replace all text with NULLs to avoid unstructured data complexity

questions.question: You are asked to perform data mining on thousands of customer support tickets (free-text) to discover common issues and trends. Which workflow is most appropriate?
